behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is primarily data/state handoff across dispatcher + reviewer adapter modules. REAL-INFRA: behavior text is about payload wiring, not real HTTP/OS effects. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model judgment quality, only invocation inputs. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior is limited to 'sends ... correct ... instruction pack' (input contract), not reviewer output quality or external side-effect readback.
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatcher with in-process fakes for the three reviewer clients; assert each call received the identical document bytes/id plus the role-specific instruction pack expected for extractor, risk, and compliance.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on what the live AI extractor actually identifies; stubbed outputs would hide extraction-quality failures. MULTI-STEP: single reviewer decision, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly says 'live clause extractor identifies key terms,' which is model-output quality, not just routing/retry/payload format.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real clause-extractor model on representative contracts and score whether required term classes are correctly identified (precision/recall or acceptance thresholds), including varied wording.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in module wiring (policy config propagation), not single-function logic only. REAL-INFRA: no requirement for real network/subprocess behavior. LLM-DEP: does not assert risk-quality output, only that config reaches invocation. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is 'configuration is passed correctly,' explicitly an orchestration/input-contract check.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the risk-assessor invocation path with an instrumented fake client and verify the outbound request contains the exact configured policy fields, versions, and overrides.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: detection of indirect/euphemistic risky language is model-judgment quality and can vary with real model behavior. Mocks would likely always pass and hide this failure class. MULTI-STEP: one model decision path, so Agentic not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly tests semantic flagging quality ('including indirect or euphemistic phrasing').
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk-assessor model on curated contracts containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses; verify policy-aligned flags meet acceptance thresholds and include expected high-risk findings.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module parameter handoff (orchestration to compliance adapter). REAL-INFRA: no explicit need for real HTTP/OS/subprocess to validate this behavior. LLM-DEP: not about correctness of compliance judgment, only invocation inputs. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/System because behavior text is limited to 'passed correctly ... invocation.'
behaviors.test_description: Invoke compliance-checker pipeline with test jurisdiction/profile configs and assert outbound request payload contains exact jurisdiction code, regulatory profile identifiers, and effective dates.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior depends on real AI reviewer’s compliance determination quality against regulations; stubs cannot validate this. MULTI-STEP: single reviewer output quality check, so Agentic over Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires the 'live compliance checker correctly determines' outcome quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real compliance-checker model on labeled contracts spanning compliant/non-compliant cases per configured regulations; assert decision accuracy and critical requirement-level recall meet thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: key risk is schema/identity handoff between reviewer outputs and merger, which appears when modules are wired together. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network requirement in behavior text. LLM-DEP: correctness criterion is deterministic merge policy (preserve both + attribution), not model quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior targets merge mechanics after outputs exist; text does not require validating whether model assessments are themselves correct.
behaviors.test_description: Feed merger through the real in-process orchestration path with two reviewer-output fixtures that conflict on the same clause id/span; assert unified report retains both assessments and reviewer attribution fields without overwrite.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: requires multiple real agentic steps (three AI reviewers) followed by merge; correctness depends on sequenced agentic handoff and aggregation. REAL-INFRA may also apply if external calls are real, but Workflow is the higher needed check. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic-only because behavior explicitly spans full pipeline: 'dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report' (multi-step orchestration).
behaviors.test_description: Run full production-like pipeline on real contracts using live reviewer calls; verify final merged report contains every finding from each reviewer (round-trip reconciliation against per-reviewer raw outputs) with no dropped entries.
